mnemonics: Aggregate Fact Tables are like SUMmarizing reports for the BOSS (Business-Oriented Summary Storage).
keyPoints: Aggregate fact tables contain pre-summarized data to improve query performance.
They are created by rolling up granular data from a base fact table to a higher level of a dimension (e.g., daily sales → monthly sales).
They significantly reduce the amount of data that needs to be scanned for common, high-level queries.
commonMistakes: Creating aggregates for every possible dimension combination, which leads to excessive storage use and maintenance.
Forgetting to maintain the aggregate tables when the base fact table is updated, leading to inconsistent data.
